module_doc:
BUG: Download page serves SGMETA envelope bytes in the downloaded file.

Observed at: /en-gb/download/#<friendly-token>

Symptom (confirmed by QA, 31 March 2026):
  The download page decrypts the transfer correctly, but the file handed to
  the browser contains the raw SGMETA envelope prepended to the file content:

      SGMETA{"filename": "vault-snapshot.zip"}PK...

  The user receives a corrupt file that is the SGMETA-wrapped payload, not
  the original file.

Root cause (suspected):
  The download page's decrypt path calls something like:
      blob = new Blob([decryptedBuffer]);
  rather than:
      blob = new Blob([stripSGMETA(decryptedBuffer)]);

  The SGMETA format is:
      magic(6 bytes: 0x53 0x47 0x4D 0x45 0x54 0x41)
    + length(4 bytes big-endian: byte count of JSON metadata)
    + metadata(JSON: {"filename": "..."})
    + file_content

  The download page must parse and discard the header before creating the Blob.

Fix needed in: send-download.js or the download page's decrypt callback.
  After decryption, parse the SGMETA header and use only the content slice.

This test PASSES while the bug exists (download starts with SGMETA magic).
When fixed, the download will NOT start with SGMETA → this test FAILS →
delete this file and add a proper positive assertion to the download test suite.

tests.doc:
BUG: Downloaded file starts with SGMETA magic bytes (envelope not stripped).

        PASSES while the bug exists.
        FAILS when fixed → delete this file.

BUG: The actual file content is present but buried after the SGMETA header.

        The content is not lost — it's just wrapped. Confirms the bug is purely
        a failure to strip the envelope, not a decryption error.

        PASSES while the bug exists.
        FAILS when fixed → delete this file.
